layman_headline: Fear grips the crypto market — no clear swing trade setup stands out right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin dominance high at 56.6%, which typically means altcoins underperform. Most of the swing candidates are either in strong downtrends with bearish chart structures, wildly overbought (PRIME at RSI 95), or are questionable micro-cap tokens with unreliable data (GROK showing -100%). The best relative candidate is Monad (MON), which shows a bullish engulfing candle and decent risk/reward at 2.87x, but it's still below all major moving averages in a downtrend. The setup isn't compelling enough to commit capital right now.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is fearful but hasn't shown a clear reversal signal yet. Watch for Fear & Greed to dip below 20 (extreme fear) or for BTC to stabilize and form a higher low — that would give altcoins room to bounce.
expert_reasoning: Scanning all candidates: BCAP is a 363% pump with zero technicals — classic chase trap. GROK shows -100% change which appears to be a data anomaly or rug. PRIME has RSI at 95.34 — extremely overbought despite bullish chart, making it a sell not a buy. BONK has RSI 29 (oversold, a reliable signal historically) but the chart bias is bearish at -0.70 with no support level identified and a downtrend. NIGHT is similarly oversold but in a steep downtrend with bearish structure. MON stands out with a bullish engulfing candle, RSI 42 approaching oversold, and a 2.87x risk/reward ratio to nearest support/resistance. However, price is below all MAs in a downtrend, and BTC dominance at 56.6% suppresses alt rallies. Fear & Greed at 31 is improving (from 28.3 weekly avg) but hasn't reached the extreme fear capitulation zone that typically produces the best bounces. No position is warranted until we see either a confirmed trend reversal in MON or a broader market fear capitulation.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.6% continues to drain capital from altcoins — alts may underperform even if BTC stabilizes
Fear & Greed at 31 is improving but hasn't reached extreme fear levels where the highest-conviction bounce trades emerge
Most candidates lack sufficient chart data, volume metrics, or funding rate data to build high-conviction setups
watch_list: Fear & Greed index — watch for a drop below 20 (extreme fear) which historically produces the best mean-reversion entries
MON price action — if it holds above $0.020 support and prints another bullish candle, the engulfing pattern strengthens
BONK RSI — at 29 it's oversold; if RSI dips below 25 with a volume spike, that could be a capitulation bounce opportunity
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear regime (F&G 31) with BTC at $64.7K showing mild weakness (-1.9% 24h). High BTC dominance at 56.6% signals capital rotation away from alts, making altcoin swing trades particularly risky right now.
context_banner: Market fear is improving but not yet at capitulation levels — patience is the highest-conviction trade today.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 31 (Fear) improving from weekly avg of 28.3, but not yet at extreme fear levels that produce reliable bounce entries.
BONK RSI at 29 is oversold (historically 83% win rate signal), but bearish chart structure (-0.70 bias) and no visible support make it too risky to act on alone.
MON shows a bullish engulfing candle with 2.87x risk/reward, but price below all MAs in a downtrend prevents confirmation.
PRIME RSI at 95.34 is extremely overbought — despite bullish chart, this signals imminent correction risk and disqualifies it as a buy candidate.
Ethereum nearing market bottom vs BTC per CryptoQuant, but key signals remain unconfirmed — supports waiting rather than acting prematurely on ETH or ETH-ecosystem plays.
